video_title: What Is Deep Learning and Why Now?
learning_objectives: Define deep learning as learning useful representations and mappings from complex data; Distinguish artificial intelligence machine learning and deep learning; Explain the ABCD framework: algorithms big data computing and domain knowledge; Recognize common predictive and generative deep learning input-output tasks
natural_stopping_point_explanation: The lecture reaches a natural close on slide 21 after moving from motivation and historical context through enabling conditions neural-network basics application examples and a final summary definition.
suggested_recap_slide_idea: Recap the module with four anchors: AI/ML/DL relationship; why deep learning took off now; neural networks as parameterized input-output functions; predictive versus generative mappings.
suggested_quiz_checkpoint_question: Checkpoint: In one or two sentences explain how deep learning differs from traditional machine learning and name two conditions that made modern deep learning practical.
